speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Thrice the brinded cat hath mewed.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Thrice, and once the hedge-pig whined.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Harpier cries “’Tis time, ’tis time!”

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Round about the cauldron go;

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: In the poisoned entrails throw.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Toad, that under cold stone

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Days and nights has thirty-one

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Sweltered venom sleeping got,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Boil thou first i’ th’ charmèd pot.

speaker: ALL
text: Double, double toil and trouble;

speaker: ALL
text: Fire burn, and cauldron bubble.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Fillet of a fenny snake

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: In the cauldron boil and bake.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Eye of newt and toe of frog,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Wool of bat and tongue of dog,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Adder’s fork and blindworm’s sting,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Lizard’s leg and howlet’s wing,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: For a charm of powerful trouble,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Like a hell-broth boil and bubble.

speaker: ALL
text: Double, double toil and trouble;

speaker: ALL
text: Fire burn, and cauldron bubble.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Scale of dragon, tooth of wolf,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Witch’s mummy, maw and gulf

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Of the ravined salt-sea shark,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Root of hemlock digged i’ th’ dark,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Liver of blaspheming Jew,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Gall of goat and slips of yew

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Slivered in the moon’s eclipse,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Nose of Turk and Tartar’s lips,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Finger of birth-strangled babe

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Ditch-delivered by a drab,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Make the gruel thick and slab.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Add thereto a tiger’s chaudron

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: For th’ ingredience of our cauldron.

speaker: ALL
text: Double, double toil and trouble;

speaker: ALL
text: Fire burn, and cauldron bubble.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Cool it with a baboon’s blood.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Then the charm is firm and good.

speaker: HECATE
text: O, well done! I commend your pains,

speaker: HECATE
text: And everyone shall share i’ th’ gains.

speaker: HECATE
text: And now about the cauldron sing

speaker: HECATE
text: Like elves and fairies in a ring,

speaker: HECATE
text: Enchanting all that you put in.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: By the pricking of my thumbs,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Something wicked this way comes.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Open, locks,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Whoever knocks.

speaker: MACBETH
text: How now, you secret, black, and midnight hags?

speaker: MACBETH
text: What is ’t you do?

speaker: ALL
text: A deed without a name.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I conjure you by that which you profess

speaker: MACBETH
text: (Howe’er you come to know it), answer me.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Though you untie the winds and let them fight

speaker: MACBETH
text: Against the churches, though the yeasty waves

speaker: MACBETH
text: Confound and swallow navigation up,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Though bladed corn be lodged and trees blown down,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Though castles topple on their warders’ heads,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Though palaces and pyramids do slope

speaker: MACBETH
text: Their heads to their foundations, though the treasure

speaker: MACBETH
text: Of nature’s germens tumble all together

speaker: MACBETH
text: Even till destruction sicken, answer me

speaker: MACBETH
text: To what I ask you.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Speak.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Demand.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: We’ll answer.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Say if th’ hadst rather hear it from our mouths

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Or from our masters’.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Call ’em. Let me see ’em.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Pour in sow’s blood that hath eaten

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Her nine farrow; grease that’s sweaten

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: From the murderers’ gibbet throw

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Into the flame.

speaker: ALL
text: Come high or low;

speaker: ALL
text: Thyself and office deftly show.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Tell me, thou unknown power—

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: He knows thy thought.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Hear his speech but say thou naught.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              APPARITION
text: Macbeth! Macbeth! Macbeth! Beware Macduff!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              APPARITION
text: Beware the Thane of Fife! Dismiss me. Enough.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Whate’er thou art, for thy good caution, thanks.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thou hast harped my fear aright. But one word more—

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: He will not be commanded. Here’s another

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: More potent than the first.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              APPARITION
text: Macbeth! Macbeth! Macbeth!—

speaker: MACBETH
text: Had I three ears, I’d hear thee.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              APPARITION
text: Be bloody, bold, and resolute. Laugh to scorn

speaker:
SECOND
               
              APPARITION
text: The power of man, for none of woman born

speaker:
SECOND
               
              APPARITION
text: Shall harm Macbeth.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Then live, Macduff; what need I fear of thee?

speaker: MACBETH
text: But yet I’ll make assurance double sure

speaker: MACBETH
text: And take a bond of fate. Thou shalt not live,

speaker: MACBETH
text: That I may tell pale-hearted fear it lies,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And sleep in spite of thunder.

speaker: MACBETH
text: What is this

speaker: MACBETH
text: That rises like the issue of a king

speaker: MACBETH
text: And wears upon his baby brow the round

speaker: MACBETH
text: And top of sovereignty?

speaker: ALL
text: Listen but speak not to ’t.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              APPARITION
text: Be lion-mettled, proud, and take no care

speaker:
THIRD
               
              APPARITION
text: Who chafes, who frets, or where conspirers are.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              APPARITION
text: Macbeth shall never vanquished be until

speaker:
THIRD
               
              APPARITION
text: Great Birnam Wood to high Dunsinane Hill

speaker:
THIRD
               
              APPARITION
text: Shall come against him.

speaker: MACBETH
text: That will never be.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Who can impress the forest, bid the tree

speaker: MACBETH
text: Unfix his earthbound root? Sweet bodements, good!

speaker: MACBETH
text: Rebellious dead, rise never till the Wood

speaker: MACBETH
text: Of Birnam rise, and our high-placed Macbeth

speaker: MACBETH
text: Shall live the lease of nature, pay his breath

speaker: MACBETH
text: To time and mortal custom. Yet my heart

speaker: MACBETH
text: Throbs to know one thing. Tell me, if your art

speaker: MACBETH
text: Can tell so much: shall Banquo’s issue ever

speaker: MACBETH
text: Reign in this kingdom?

speaker: ALL
text: Seek to know no more.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I will be satisfied. Deny me this,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And an eternal curse fall on you! Let me know!

speaker: MACBETH
text: Why sinks that cauldron? And what noise is this?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Show.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Show.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Show.

speaker: ALL
text: Show his eyes and grieve his heart.

speaker: ALL
text: Come like shadows; so depart.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thou art too like the spirit of Banquo. Down!

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thy crown does sear mine eyeballs. And thy hair,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thou other gold-bound brow, is like the first.

speaker: MACBETH
text: A third is like the former.—Filthy hags,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Why do you show me this?—A fourth? Start, eyes!

speaker: MACBETH
text: What, will the line stretch out to th’ crack of doom?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Another yet? A seventh? I’ll see no more.

speaker: MACBETH
text: And yet the eighth appears who bears a glass

speaker: MACBETH
text: Which shows me many more, and some I see

speaker: MACBETH
text: That twofold balls and treble scepters carry.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Horrible sight! Now I see ’tis true,

speaker: MACBETH
text: For the blood-boltered Banquo smiles upon me

speaker: MACBETH
text: And points at them for his.

speaker: MACBETH
text: What, is this so?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Ay, sir, all this is so. But why

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Stands Macbeth thus amazedly?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Come, sisters, cheer we up his sprites

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: And show the best of our delights.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: I’ll charm the air to give a sound

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: While you perform your antic round,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: That this great king may kindly say

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Our duties did his welcome pay.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Where are they? Gone? Let this pernicious hour

speaker: MACBETH
text: Stand aye accursèd in the calendar!—

speaker: MACBETH
text: Come in, without there.

speaker: LENNOX
text: What’s your Grace’s will?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Saw you the Weïrd Sisters?

speaker: LENNOX
text: No, my lord.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Came they not by you?

speaker: LENNOX
text: No, indeed, my lord.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Infected be the air whereon they ride,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And damned all those that trust them! I did hear

speaker: MACBETH
text: The galloping of horse. Who was ’t came by?

speaker: LENNOX
text: ’Tis two or three, my lord, that bring you word

speaker: LENNOX
text: Macduff is fled to England.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Fled to England?

speaker: LENNOX
text: Ay, my good lord.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Time, thou anticipat’st my dread exploits.

speaker: MACBETH
text: The flighty purpose never is o’ertook

speaker: MACBETH
text: Unless the deed go with it. From this moment

speaker: MACBETH
text: The very firstlings of my heart shall be

speaker: MACBETH
text: The firstlings of my hand. And even now,

speaker: MACBETH
text: To crown my thoughts with acts, be it thought and done:

speaker: MACBETH
text: The castle of Macduff I will surprise,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Seize upon Fife, give to th’ edge o’ th’ sword

speaker: MACBETH
text: His wife, his babes, and all unfortunate souls

speaker: MACBETH
text: That trace him in his line. No boasting like a fool;

speaker: MACBETH
text: This deed I’ll do before this purpose cool.

speaker: MACBETH
text: But no more sights!—Where are these gentlemen?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Come bring me where they are.
